name: Lake Texoma
description:
Lake Texoma is one of the largest reservoirs in the US, straddling the Oklahoma-Texas border. Famous for its naturally reproducing striped bass population — one of the best striper fisheries in the country.

The lake covers 89,000 acres fed by the Red and Washita Rivers. Besides stripers, excellent smallmouth bass fishing along rocky Oklahoma shoreline and largemouth in Texas coves.

Denison and Sherman on the Texas side offer full services. The lake draws anglers from both DFW and Oklahoma City.

name: Lake Fork Reservoir
description:
Lake Fork is Texas's #1 trophy bass lake. It has produced more ShareLunker bass (13+ pounds) than any other lake in the state. The flooded timber and managed habitat create ideal conditions for growing giant largemouth.

The lake's slot limit (16-24 inches must be released) has helped build and maintain the trophy fishery. Spring is prime time, but quality fish are caught year-round.

Small towns around the lake — Quitman, Emory, Alba — cater heavily to anglers with bait shops, guides, and cabin rentals.

name: Sam Rayburn Reservoir
description:
Sam Rayburn is the largest lake entirely within Texas at 114,500 acres. Located in the Piney Woods of East Texas, it offers outstanding bass fishing in flooded timber and standing dead trees.

The sheer size means you can always find unpressured water. Crappie fishing is also excellent, especially in winter and spring around submerged brush.

name: Toledo Bend Reservoir
description:
Toledo Bend is one of the largest man-made lakes in the US at 181,600 acres, spanning the Texas-Louisiana border. It's a legendary bass fishing destination that has hosted major tournaments.

The lake's massive size and diverse habitat produce quality bass throughout the year. Standing timber, grass beds, and rocky points all hold fish.

name: Lake Conroe
description:
Lake Conroe is the premier fishing and recreation lake in the Houston metro area. Just 40 miles north of downtown Houston, it offers excellent bass fishing along its many residential docks and natural shoreline.

The lake supports good populations of largemouth bass, catfish, crappie, and hybrid stripers.

name: Lake Travis
description:
Lake Travis is Austin's playground — a clear Highland Lakes reservoir in the Texas Hill Country. The lake is famous for its deep, clear water and scenic limestone cliffs.

Guadalupe bass (Texas's state fish), largemouth, and stripers are the primary game fish. The lake's clear water makes sight-fishing possible.

name: Possum Kingdom Lake
description:
Possum Kingdom Lake is one of the clearest lakes in Texas with visibility up to 20 feet. Known for trophy striped bass and scenic bluffs, it sits in the Palo Pinto Mountains west of Fort Worth.

The lake's Hell's Gate rock formation is one of the most photographed landmarks in Texas.

name: Lake Whitney
description: Lake Whitney is a large Corps of Engineers lake on the Brazos River, known for excellent striped bass and smallmouth bass fishing. The rocky limestone bluffs provide ideal smallmouth habitat.

name: Lewisville Lake
description: Lewisville Lake is the DFW metroplex's most accessible big lake, just north of Dallas. It offers solid bass and crappie fishing with numerous public ramps and parks along its shores.

name: Lake Ray Roberts
description: Lake Ray Roberts is a well-managed Corps of Engineers lake north of Denton with excellent bass fishing. The state park on its shores provides great camping and ramp access.

name: Lake Livingston
description: Lake Livingston is one of the largest lakes in Texas at 83,000 acres. It's the catfish capital of East Texas and also offers excellent white bass runs up the Trinity River in spring.

name: Falcon Lake
description: Falcon Lake on the US-Mexico border is legendary for trophy largemouth bass. The warm South Texas climate allows bass to grow year-round, producing 10+ pound fish regularly.

name: Lake Amistad
description: Lake Amistad is a crystal-clear international reservoir on the Rio Grande near Del Rio. The clear water supports both largemouth and smallmouth bass, plus excellent striped bass fishing in deep water.

name: Canyon Lake
description: Canyon Lake is a clear Hill Country lake between San Antonio and Austin. The tailrace below the dam is one of the best trout fisheries in Texas, and the lake itself produces quality bass and stripers.

name: Cedar Creek Lake
description: Cedar Creek Lake is a popular DFW-area fishing and recreation lake east of Dallas. Good bass, catfish, and crappie fishing in a lakeside community atmosphere.

name: Lake Tawakoni
description: Lake Tawakoni is a large, productive lake east of Dallas with diverse fishing opportunities. Blue catfish over 50 pounds are caught regularly, and the bass fishing is underrated.

name: Lake Buchanan
description: Lake Buchanan is the largest and northernmost of the Highland Lakes chain in the Texas Hill Country. Trophy striped bass are the main draw, with fish over 30 pounds caught each year.
